steps.title: Convert radical to exponential form
steps.content: Recall the property $\sqrt[n]{x} = x^{1/n}$. Each choice has the form $\sqrt[n]{a^{132}} = (a^{132})^{1/n} = a^{132/n}$. We need to find which index $n$ makes $a^{132/n} = a^{11/12}$.

steps.title: Solve for the index by matching exponents
steps.content:
Since the bases are the same, the exponents must be equal:

$$\frac{132}{n} = \frac{11}{12}$$

Observe that $132 = 11 \times 12$. For the equation to hold:

$$\frac{11 \times 12}{n} = \frac{11}{12}$$

This is true when $n = 12 \times 12 = 144$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
